 172        private ContractDescription LoadOutputChannelContractDescription()
 173        {
 0174            Type channelType = typeof(IOutputChannel);
 0175            XmlQualifiedName contractName = NamingHelper.GetContractName(channelType, null, NamingHelper.MSNamespace);
 0176            ContractDescription contract = new ContractDescription(contractName.Name, contractName.Namespace)
 0177            {
 0178                ContractType = channelType,
 0179                ConfigurationName = channelType.FullName,
 0180                SessionMode = SessionMode.NotAllowed
 0181            };
 0182            OperationDescription operation = new OperationDescription("Send", contract);
 0183            MessageDescription message = new MessageDescription(MessageHeaders.WildcardAction, MessageDirection.Input);
 0184            operation.Messages.Add(message);
 0185            contract.Operations.Add(operation);
 0186            return contract;
 187        }
 188
 189        private ContractDescription LoadRequestChannelContractDescription()
 190        {
 0191            Type channelType = typeof(IRequestChannel);
 0192            XmlQualifiedName contractName = NamingHelper.GetContractName(channelType, null, NamingHelper.MSNamespace);
 0193            ContractDescription contract = new ContractDescription(contractName.Name, contractName.Namespace)
 0194            {
 0195                ContractType = channelType,
 0196                ConfigurationName = channelType.FullName,
 0197                SessionMode = SessionMode.NotAllowed
 0198            };
 0199            OperationDescription operation = new OperationDescription("Request", contract);
 0200            MessageDescription request = new MessageDescription(MessageHeaders.WildcardAction, MessageDirection.Input);
 0201            MessageDescription reply = new MessageDescription(MessageHeaders.WildcardAction, MessageDirection.Output);
 0202            operation.Messages.Add(request);
 0203            operation.Messages.Add(reply);
 0204            contract.Operations.Add(operation);
 0205            return contract;
 206        }

 384                        {
 0385                            operationDescription.KnownTypes.Add(knownType);
 386                        }
 387                    }
 388                }
 389            }
 641390        }

 395            {

 398                {
 2399                    Type type = knownTypeAttribute.DeclaringType;

 401                    {
 0402                        type = (provider as TypeInfo)?.AsType();

 0404                            type = ((MethodInfo)provider).DeclaringType;
 405                    }
 2406                    MethodInfo method = type.GetMethod(knownTypeAttribute.MethodName, BindingFlags.Static | BindingFlags

 409

 0411                        throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(new InvalidOperationException(SR.Forma
 412
 2413                    return (IEnumerable<Type>)method.Invoke(null, new object[] { provider });
 414                }
 415            }
 416
 2993417            List<Type> knownTypes = new List<Type>();
 6060418            for (int i = 0; i < knownTypeAttributes.Length; ++i)
 419            {
 37420                ServiceKnownTypeAttribute knownTypeAttribute = (ServiceKnownTypeAttribute)knownTypeAttributes[i];

 422                {
 0423                    throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(new InvalidOperationException(SR.Format(SR
 424                }

 37426                knownTypes.Add(knownTypeAttribute.Type);
 427            }
 2993428            return knownTypes;

 431        private KeyedByTypeCollection<TOperation> GetIOperationAttributesFromType<TOperation>(OperationDescription opDes
 432        {
 12190433            var result = new KeyedByTypeCollection<TOperation>();
 12190434            var ifaceMap = default(InterfaceMapping);
 12190435            bool useImplAttrs = false;
 12190436            if (implType != null)
 437            {
 9839438                if (targetIface.IsAssignableFrom(implType) && targetIface.IsInterface)
 439                {
 440                    // if implType is an interface not an implementation class
 441                    // we cannot use the interface map for looking up the implementation methods
 4962442                    if (!implType.IsInterface)
 443                    {
 4944444                        ifaceMap = implType.GetInterfaceMap(targetIface);
 4944445                        useImplAttrs = true;
 446                    }
 447                }
 448                else
 449                {
 450                    // implType does not implement any methods from the targetIface, so there is nothing to do
 4877451                    return result;
 452                }
 453            }
 7313454            MethodInfo opMethod = opDesc.OperationMethod;
 7313455            ProcessOpMethod(opMethod, true, opDesc, result, ifaceMap, useImplAttrs, attributeMustBeOnImplType ? implType
 7313456            if (opDesc.SyncMethod != null && opDesc.BeginMethod != null)
 457            {
 0458                ProcessOpMethod(opDesc.BeginMethod, false, opDesc, result, ifaceMap, useImplAttrs, attributeMustBeOnImpl
 459            }
 7313460            else if (opDesc.SyncMethod != null && opDesc.TaskMethod != null)
 461            {
 0462                ProcessOpMethod(opDesc.TaskMethod, false, opDesc, result, ifaceMap, useImplAttrs, attributeMustBeOnImplT
 463            }
 7313464            else if (opDesc.TaskMethod != null && opDesc.BeginMethod != null)
 465            {
 0466                ProcessOpMethod(opDesc.BeginMethod, false, opDesc, result, ifaceMap, useImplAttrs, attributeMustBeOnImpl
 467            }
 7313468            return result;
 469        }
 470
 471        private void ProcessOpMethod<IOperation>(MethodInfo opMethod, bool canHaveBehaviors,
 472                             OperationDescription opDesc, KeyedByTypeCollection<IOperation> result,
 473                             InterfaceMapping ifaceMap, bool useImplAttrs, Type implType)
 474        {
 7313475            MethodInfo method = null;
 7313476            if (useImplAttrs)
 477            {
 4944478                int methodIndex = Array.IndexOf(ifaceMap.InterfaceMethods, opMethod);
 479                // if opMethod doesn't exist in the interfacemap, it means opMethod was on
 480                // the "other" interface (not the one implemented by implType)
 4944481                if (methodIndex != -1)
 482                {
 4938483                    MethodInfo implMethod = ifaceMap.TargetMethods[methodIndex];
 484                    // C++ allows you to create abstract classes that have missing interface method
 485                    // implementations, which shows up as nulls in the interfacemapping
 4938486                    if (implMethod != null && (implType == null || implMethod.DeclaringType == implType))
 487                    {
 4868488                        method = implMethod;
 489                    }
 490                }
 4944491                if (method == null)
 492                {
 76493                    return;
 494                }
 495            }
 496            else
 497            {
 2369498                method = opMethod;
 499            }
 500
 7237501            object[] methodAttributes = ServiceReflector.GetCustomAttributes(method, typeof(IOperation), false);
 15306502            for (int k = 0; k < methodAttributes.Length; k++)
 503            {
 416504                IOperation opBehaviorAttr = (IOperation)methodAttributes[k];
 416505                if (canHaveBehaviors)
 506                {
 416507                    result.Add(opBehaviorAttr);
 508                }
 509                else
 510                {
 0511                    if (opDesc.SyncMethod != null && opDesc.BeginMethod != null)
 512                    {
 0513                        throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(
 0514                            new InvalidOperationException(SR.Format(SR.SyncAsyncMatchConsistency_Attributes6,
 0515                                                                       opDesc.SyncMethod.Name,
 0516                                                                       opDesc.SyncMethod.DeclaringType,
 0517                                                                       opDesc.BeginMethod.Name,
 0518                                                                       opDesc.EndMethod.Name,
 0519                                                                       opDesc.Name,
 0520                                                                       opBehaviorAttr.GetType().FullName)));
 521                    }
 0522                    else if (opDesc.SyncMethod != null && opDesc.TaskMethod != null)
 523                    {
 0524                        throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(
 0525                            new InvalidOperationException(SR.Format(SR.SyncTaskMatchConsistency_Attributes6,
 0526                                                                       opDesc.SyncMethod.Name,
 0527                                                                       opDesc.SyncMethod.DeclaringType,
 0528                                                                       opDesc.TaskMethod.Name,
 0529                                                                       opDesc.Name,
 0530                                                                       opBehaviorAttr.GetType().FullName)));
 531                    }
 0532                    else if (opDesc.TaskMethod != null && opDesc.BeginMethod != null)
 533                    {
 0534                        throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(
 0535                            new InvalidOperationException(SR.Format(SR.TaskAsyncMatchConsistency_Attributes6,
 0536                                                                       opDesc.TaskMethod.Name,
 0537                                                                       opDesc.TaskMethod.DeclaringType,
 0538                                                                       opDesc.BeginMethod.Name,
 0539                                                                       opDesc.EndMethod.Name,
 0540                                                                       opDesc.Name,
 0541                                                                       opBehaviorAttr.GetType().FullName)));
 542                    }
 543                    Fx.Assert("Invalid state. No exception for canHaveBehaviors = false");
 544                }
 545            }
 7237546        }
 547
 548        // Returns true if the given methods match in name and parameter types
 549        private static bool MethodsMatch(MethodInfo method1, MethodInfo method2)
 550        {
 551            Contract.Assert(method1 != null);
 552            Contract.Assert(method2 != null);
 553
 0554            if (method1.Equals(method2))
 555            {
 0556                return true;
 557            }
 558
 0559            if (method1.ReturnType != method2.ReturnType ||
 0560                !string.Equals(method1.Name, method2.Name, StringComparison.Ordinal) ||
 0561                !ParameterInfosMatch(method1.ReturnParameter, method2.ReturnParameter))
 562            {
 0563                return false;
 564            }
 565
 0566            ParameterInfo[] parameters1 = method1.GetParameters();
 0567            ParameterInfo[] parameters2 = method2.GetParameters();
 0568            if (parameters1.Length != parameters2.Length)
 569            {
 0570                return false;
 571            }
 572
 0573            for (int i = 0; i < parameters1.Length; ++i)
 574            {
 0575                if (!ParameterInfosMatch(parameters1[i], parameters2[i]))
 576                {
 0577                    return false;
 578                }
 579            }
 580
 0581            return true;
 582        }
 583
 584        // Returns true if 2 ParameterInfo's match in signature with respect
 585        // to the MemberInfo's in which they are declared. Position is required
 586        // to match but name is not.
 587        private static bool ParameterInfosMatch(ParameterInfo parameterInfo1, ParameterInfo parameterInfo2)
 588        {
 589            // Null is possible for a ParameterInfo from MethodInfo.ReturnParameter.
 590            // If both are null, we have no information to compare and say they are equal.
 0591            if (parameterInfo1 == null && parameterInfo2 == null)
 592            {
 0593                return true;
 594            }
 595
 0596            if (parameterInfo1 == null || parameterInfo2 == null)
 597            {
 0598                return false;
 599            }
 600
 0601            if (parameterInfo1.Equals(parameterInfo2))
 602            {
 0603                return true;
 604            }
 605
 0606            return ((parameterInfo1.ParameterType == parameterInfo2.ParameterType) &&
 0607                    (parameterInfo1.IsIn == parameterInfo2.IsIn) &&
 0608                    (parameterInfo1.IsOut == parameterInfo2.IsOut) &&
 0609                    (parameterInfo1.IsRetval == parameterInfo2.IsRetval) &&
 0610                    (parameterInfo1.Position == parameterInfo2.Position));
 611        }
 612

 673        internal static int CompareMessagePartDescriptions(MessagePartDescription a, MessagePartDescription b)
 674        {
 9675            int posCmp = a.SerializationPosition - b.SerializationPosition;
 9676            if (posCmp != 0)
 677            {
 0678                return posCmp;
 679            }
 680
 9681            int nsCmp = string.Compare(a.Namespace, b.Namespace, StringComparison.Ordinal);
 9682            if (nsCmp != 0)
 683            {
 0684                return nsCmp;
 685            }
 686
 9687            return string.Compare(a.Name, b.Name, StringComparison.Ordinal);
 688        }
 689
 690        internal static XmlName GetBodyWrapperResponseName(string operationName)
 691        {
 692#if DEBUG
 693            Fx.Assert(NamingHelper.IsValidNCName(operationName), "operationName value has to be a valid NCName.");
 694#endif
 0695            return new XmlName(operationName + ResponseSuffix);
 696        }
 697
 698        internal static XmlName GetBodyWrapperResponseName(XmlName operationName)
 699        {
 2351700            return new XmlName(operationName.EncodedName + ResponseSuffix, true /*isEncoded*/);
 701        }

 703        private void CreateOperationDescriptions(ContractDescription contractDescription,
 704                                         ContractReflectionInfo reflectionInfo,
 705                                         Type contractToGetMethodsFrom,
 706                                         ContractDescription declaringContract,
 707                                         MessageDirection direction
 708                                         )
 709        {
 646710            MessageDirection otherDirection = MessageDirectionHelper.Opposite(direction);
 646711            if (!(declaringContract.ContractType.IsAssignableFrom(contractDescription.ContractType)))
 712            {
 713                Fx.Assert("bad contract inheritance");
 0714                throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(new InvalidOperationException(
 0715                    string.Format(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, "Bad contract inheritance. Contract {0} does not impleme
 0716                    ));
 717            }
 718
 6032719            foreach (MethodInfo methodInfo in contractToGetMethodsFrom.GetMethods(TypeLoader.DefaultBindingFlags))
 720            {
 2371721                if (contractToGetMethodsFrom.IsInterface)
 722                {
 2350723                    object[] attrs = ServiceReflector.GetCustomAttributes(methodInfo, typeof(OperationBehaviorAttribute)
 2348724                    if (attrs.Length != 0)
 725                    {
 0726                        throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(new InvalidOperationException(
 0727                            SR.Format(SR.SFxOperationBehaviorAttributeOnlyOnServiceClass, methodInfo.Name, contractToGet
 728                    }
 729                }
 2369730                ServiceReflector.ValidateParameterMetadata(methodInfo);
 2369731                OperationDescription operation = CreateOperationDescription(contractDescription, methodInfo, direction, 
 2369732                if (operation != null)
 733                {
 2351734                    contractDescription.Operations.Add(operation);
 735                }
 736            }
 644737        }
 738
 739        //Checks whether that the Callback contract provided on a ServiceContract follows rules
 740        //1. It has to be a interface
 741        //2. If its a class then it needs to implement MarshallByRefObject
 742        internal static void EnsureCallbackType(Type callbackType)
 743        {
 643744            if (callbackType != null && !callbackType.IsInterface && !callbackType.IsMarshalByRef)
 745            {
 0746                throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperArgument(SR.Format(SR.SFxInvalidCallbackContractType
 747            }
 643748        }
 749
 750        // checks a contract for substitutability (in the Liskov Substitution Principle sense), throws on error
 751        internal static void EnsureSubcontract(ServiceContractAttribute svcContractAttr, Type contractType)
 752        {
 643753            Type callbackType = svcContractAttr.CallbackContract;
 754
 643755            List<Type> types = ServiceReflector.GetInheritedContractTypes(contractType);
 1440756            for (int i = 0; i < types.Count; i++)
 757            {
 77758                Type inheritedContractType = types[i];
 77759                ServiceContractAttribute inheritedContractAttr = ServiceReflector.GetRequiredSingleAttribute<ServiceCont
 760                // we must be covariant in our callbacks
 77761                if (inheritedContractAttr.CallbackContract != null)
 762                {
 0763                    if (callbackType == null)
 764                    {
 0765                        throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(new InvalidOperationException(
 0766                            SR.Format(SR.InAContractInheritanceHierarchyIfParentHasCallbackChildMustToo,
 0767                            inheritedContractType.Name, inheritedContractAttr.CallbackContract.Name, contractType.Name))
 768                    }
 0769                    if (!inheritedContractAttr.CallbackContract.IsAssignableFrom(callbackType))
 770                    {
 0771                        throw DiagnosticUtility.ExceptionUtility.ThrowHelperError(new InvalidOperationException(
 0772                            SR.Format(SR.InAContractInheritanceHierarchyTheServiceContract3_2,
 0773                            inheritedContractType.Name, contractType.Name)));
 774                    }
 775                }
 776            }
 643777        }
 778
 779        private ContractDescription CreateContractDescription(ServiceContractAttribute contractAttr, Type contractType, 
 780        {
 643781            reflectionInfo = new ContractReflectionInfo();
 782
 643783            XmlQualifiedName contractName = NamingHelper.GetContractName(contractType, contractAttr.Name, contractAttr.N
 643784            ContractDescription contractDescription = new ContractDescription(contractName.Name, contractName.Namespace)
 643785            {
 643786                ContractType = contractType
 643787            };
 788
 789            // MessageSecurity not supported
 790            //if (contractAttr.HasProtectionLevel)
 791            //{
 792            //    contractDescription.ProtectionLevel = contractAttr.ProtectionLevel;
 793            //}
 794
 643795            Type callbackType = contractAttr.CallbackContract;
 796
 643797            EnsureCallbackType(callbackType);
 798
 643799            EnsureSubcontract(contractAttr, contractType);
 800
 801            // reflect the methods in contractType and add OperationDescriptions to ContractDescription
 643802            reflectionInfo.iface = contractType;
 643803            reflectionInfo.callbackiface = callbackType;
 804
 643805            contractDescription.SessionMode = contractAttr.SessionMode;
 643806            contractDescription.CallbackContractType = callbackType;
 643807            contractDescription.ConfigurationName = contractAttr.ConfigurationName ?? contractType.FullName;
 808
 809            // get inherited operations
 643810            List<Type> types = ServiceReflector.GetInheritedContractTypes(contractType);
 643811            List<Type> inheritedCallbackTypes = new List<Type>();
 1440812            for (int i = 0; i < types.Count; i++)
 813            {
 77814                Type inheritedContractType = types[i];
 77815                ServiceContractAttribute inheritedContractAttr = ServiceReflector.GetRequiredSingleAttribute<ServiceCont
 77816                ContractDescription inheritedContractDescription = LoadContractDescriptionHelper(inheritedContractType, 
 448817                foreach (OperationDescription op in inheritedContractDescription.Operations)
 818                {
 147819                    if (!contractDescription.Operations.Contains(op)) // in a diamond hierarchy, ensure we don't add sam
 820                    {
 821                        // ensure two different parents don't try to add conflicting operations
 147822                        Collection<OperationDescription> existingOps = contractDescription.Operations.FindAll(op.Name);
 294823                        foreach (OperationDescription existingOp in existingOps)

 871        //Sync and Async should follow the rules:
 872        //    1. Parameter match
 873        //    2. Async cannot have behaviors (verification happens later in ProcessOpMethod - behaviors haven't yet been
 874        //    3. Async cannot have known types
 875        //    4. Async cannot have known faults
 876        //    5. Sync and Async have to match on OneWay status
 877        //    6. Sync and Async have to match Action and ReplyAction
 878        private void VerifyConsistency(OperationConsistencyVerifier verifier)
 879        {
 0880            verifier.VerifyParameterLength();
 0881            verifier.VerifyParameterType();
 0882            verifier.VerifyOutParameterType();
 0883            verifier.VerifyReturnType();
 0884            verifier.VerifyFaultContractAttribute();
 0885            verifier.VerifyKnownTypeAttribute();
 0886            verifier.VerifyIsOneWayStatus();
 0887            verifier.VerifyActionAndReplyAction();
 0888        }
 889
 890        // "direction" is the "direction of the interface" (from the perspective of the server, as usual):
 891        //    proxy interface on client: MessageDirection.Input
 892        //    callback interface on client: MessageDirection.Output
 893        //    service interface (or class) on server: MessageDirection.Input
 894        //    callback interface on server: MessageDirection.Output

 2035        private class ContractReflectionInfo
 2036        {
 2037            internal Type iface;
 2038            internal Type callbackiface;
 2039        }
 2040
 2041        // This function factors out the logic of how programming model attributes interact with service inheritance.
 2042        //
 2043        // To use this, just call ApplyServiceInheritance() with
 2044        //  - the service type you want to pull behavior attributes from
 2045        //  - the "destination" behavior collection, where all the right behavior attributes should be added to
 2046        //  - a delegate
 2047        // The delegate is just a function you write that behaves like this:
 2048        //    imagine that "currentType" was the only type (imagine there was no inheritance hierarchy)
 2049        //    find desired behavior attributes on this type, and add them to "behaviors"
 2050        // ApplyServiceInheritance then uses the logic you provide for getting behavior attributes from a single type,
 2051        // and it walks the actual type hierarchy and does the inheritance/override logic for you.
 2052        public static void ApplyServiceInheritance<IBehavior, TBehaviorCollection>(
 2053                     TBehaviorCollection descriptionBehaviors,
 2054                     ServiceInheritanceCallback<IBehavior> callback)
 2055            where IBehavior : class
 2056            where TBehaviorCollection : KeyedByTypeCollection<IBehavior>
 2057        {
 2058            // work our way up the class hierarchy, looking for attributes; adding "bottom up" so that for each
 2059            // type of attribute, we only pick up the bottom-most one (the one attached to most-derived class)
 372782060            for (Type currentType = typeof(TService); currentType != null; currentType = currentType.BaseType)
 2061            {
 125192062                AddBehaviorsAtOneScope(currentType, descriptionBehaviors, callback);
 2063            }
 61202064        }
 2065
 2066        public delegate void ServiceInheritanceCallback<IBehavior>(Type currentType, KeyedByTypeCollection<IBehavior> be
 2067
 2068        // To use this, just call AddBehaviorsAtOneScope() with
 2069        //  - the type you want to pull behavior attributes from
 2070        //  - the "destination" behavior collection, where all the right behavior attributes should be added to
 2071        //  - a delegate
 2072        // The delegate is just a function you write that behaves like this:
 2073        //    imagine that "currentType" was the only type (imagine there was no inheritance hierarchy)
 2074        //    find desired behavior attributes on this type, and add them to "behaviors"
 2075        // AddBehaviorsAtOneScope then uses the logic you provide for getting behavior attributes from a single type,
 2076        // and it does the override logic for you (only add the behavior if it wasn't already in the descriptionBehavior
 2077        private static void AddBehaviorsAtOneScope<IBehavior, TBehaviorCollection>(
 2078                     Type type,
 2079                     TBehaviorCollection descriptionBehaviors,
 2080                     ServiceInheritanceCallback<IBehavior> callback)
 2081            where IBehavior : class
 2082            where TBehaviorCollection : KeyedByTypeCollection<IBehavior>
 2083        {
 155112084            KeyedByTypeCollection<IBehavior> toAdd = new KeyedByTypeCollection<IBehavior>();
 155112085            callback(type, toAdd);
 2086            // toAdd now contains the set of behaviors we'd add if this type (scope) were the only source of behaviors
 2087
 323822088            for (int i = 0; i < toAdd.Count; i++)
 2089            {
 6812090                IBehavior behavior = toAdd[i];
 6812091                if (!descriptionBehaviors.Contains(behavior.GetType()))
 2092                {
 2093                    // if we didn't already see this type of attribute at a previous scope
 2094                    // then it belongs in the final result
 6792095                    if (behavior is ServiceBehaviorAttribute || behavior is CallbackBehaviorAttribute)
 2096                    {
 3772097                        descriptionBehaviors.Insert(0, behavior);
 2098                    }
 2099                    else
 2100                    {
 3022101                        descriptionBehaviors.Add(behavior);
 2102                    }
 2103                }
 2104            }
 155102105        }
 2106    }
